revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:羽田航空神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Haneda Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:正面から見た羽田航空神社.jpg | location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q217234|lt=Ōta|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Haneda Airport|ja|羽田空港 (大田区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Airport|1=Haneda Airport|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610321}} 3-3-2, Tokyo International Airport, Terminal 1, 1st Floor | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | consecration_year=July 11, 1963 ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 38) | Annual festival=May 20 | Map=Japan Tokyo#Japan | Latitude=35 | Latitude minutes=33 | Latitude seconds=12 | Longitude=139 | Longitude minutes=46 | Longitude seconds=52 | Rituals= | Shrine Rank=Shrine within premises | prefecture={{ill|Ota|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.54866666666667|N|139.78405555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Haneda Kōkū Shrine''' ({{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located within {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|qid=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}} (Haneda Airport), Terminal 1, in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q217234|lt=Ōta City|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. As it is managed as a shrine within the premises by {{ill|Japan Airport Terminal Co., Ltd.|ja|日本空港ビルデング株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Airport Terminal Co., Ltd.|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it does not possess a religious juridical person status.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=羽田空港に知る人ぞ知るパワースポット、なぜこんな所に…「落ちませんように」と受験生も|url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20211205-OYT1T50161/ |publisher=Yomiuri Shimbun Online|accessdate=2022-03-24}}</ref>
The shrine is revered by aviation workers and airport users as a protector of {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|qid=Q640853|lt=aviation safety|1=Aviation safety}} and travel safety. It is also popular among students preparing for exams, as "planes don't fall" (i.e., "don't fail").<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=東京の"落ちない神社" 羽田航空神社で合格祈願を！受験生の皆さん、羽田空港第1ターミナル 1Fへ|url=https://murauchi.muragon.com/entry/2191.html#:~:text=%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E5%9B%BD%E9%9A%9B%E7%A9%BA%E6%B8%AF%EF%BC%88%E7%BE%BD%E7%94%B0%E7%A9%BA%E6%B8%AF,%E5%90%88%E6%A0%BC%E7%A5%88%E9%A1%98%E3%81%A7%E3%81%8A%E5%8F%82%E3%82%8A%E3%82%92%EF%BC%81 |publisher=村内のぶひろ|accessdate=2022-02-23}}</ref>
== History == In 1931 (Shōwa 6), Tokyo Airfield, the predecessor of Haneda Airport, opened in the {{ill|Haneda|ja|羽田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda|1=Haneda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610318}} area, which was then a thriving temple town around {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}}. Over time, as the area developed into Japan's primary air gateway, there arose a desire among those in the aviation industry to enshrine the spirits of those who had contributed to aviation and to pray for the future progress of the industry and aviation safety.
To commemorate the 1964 Tokyo Olympics, the old terminal building at Haneda Airport underwent expansion. On July 11, 1963, Haneda Kōkū Shrine was established on the rooftop of a newly constructed special observation gallery, with a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=spirit|1=Bunrei}} transferred from the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} in {{ill|港区|en|港区|qid=Q14219833|lt=Minato City|1=港区}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=東京国際空港ターミナル・ビル十年の歩み|publisher=日本空港ビルデング株式会社|year=1965|pages=136,142}}</ref>
Additionally, during the construction of the old terminal building in 1955, a branch shrine of Anamori Inari Shrine was enshrined on the rooftop due to its historical connection to the site. It was relocated next to Haneda Kōkū Shrine at the time of its establishment.<ref>{{Cite news|title=羽田空港旧ターミナルの鳥居 壊せば「たたりが…」で取り残し|newspaper=産経新聞|date=1993-11-16}}</ref> For nearly 30 years, the two shrines were jointly revered as protectors of Haneda Airport.<ref>{{Cite news|title=羽田の航空神社、移転先宙に浮く─旅客ターミナル引っ越し。|newspaper=日本経済新聞|date=1993-09-09}}</ref>
In 1993 (Heisei 5), when the new domestic terminal building (now Terminal 1) opened, Haneda Kōkū Shrine was relocated within the new terminal building on October 29, while the Anamori Inari branch shrine was consolidated with its main shrine on November 15.<ref>{{Cite book|title=羽田空港ターミナル60th year 史|publisher=日本空港ビルデング株式会社|year=2016|page=47}}</ref> Today, the shrine continues to host its annual festival on May 20 and New Year's ceremonies led by priests from Anamori Inari Shrine.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Instagram|url=https://www.instagram.com/p/CnBDaXBvwc0/|accessdate=2023-10-06}}</ref>
Though often confused with {{ill|Haneda Shrine|ja|羽田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Hon-Haneda|ja|本羽田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hon-Haneda|1=Hon-Haneda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520750}}, the two shrines are unrelated.
== See Also == * {{ill|Japan Airport Terminal Co., Ltd.|ja|日本空港ビルデング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Airport Terminal Co., Ltd.|1=Japan Airport Terminal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11508771}} * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} * {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}}
== External Links == * [https://tokyo-haneda.com/enjoy/recommended_place/index.html Haneda Kōkū Shrine] - Haneda Airport Official Website
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|qid=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}}. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|羽田航空神社|version=102956801|comment=Imported full ja history}}
